---
id: adr-001
title: "ADR-001: LangGraph over CrewAI"
sidebar_label: "📋 ADR-001 LangGraph"
---

import { MetaBlock, ADR, DecisionTable, InfoPanel } from '@site/src/components/PageLayouts';

<MetaBlock
  owner="Varun Pritham"
  team="AI Platform"
  status="live"
  lastUpdated="2026-05-10"
  reviewedBy="James Liu, Priya Shah"
  tags={['architecture', 'adr', 'langgraph', 'agents']}
/>

<ADR
  id="001"
  title="Use LangGraph as the multi-agent orchestration framework"
  date="2026-05-10"
  status="accepted"
  deciders={['Varun Pritham', 'James Liu', 'Priya Shah']}
  context={
    <>
      We need to orchestrate 18 specialised agents across 4 pipeline phases to convert
      MDX documentation into Slidev presentations. The framework must support a shared
      state (Blackboard pattern), sequential and conditional edges, and be testable
      locally without a hosted runtime.
    </>
  }
  decision={
    <>
      We will use <strong>LangGraph</strong> with a <code>StateGraph(PipelineState)</code>
      containing 18 nodes and 17 sequential edges. Routing is data-driven via chunk type
      labels stored in shared state — no conditional graph edges required.
      All 18 agent functions are pure Python; only 4 make LLM calls.
    </>
  }
  consequences={
    <ul>
      <li>✅ Shared <code>PipelineState</code> TypedDict is the single source of truth (Blackboard)</li>
      <li>✅ Easy to add new agents — one new node + one new edge</li>
      <li>✅ Local execution, no hosted runtime or API key required beyond the LLM</li>
      <li>⚠️ Sequential graph means no parallelism yet — Phase 1 agents could run concurrently</li>
      <li>⚠️ LangGraph API surface changes frequently between minor versions</li>
    </ul>
  }
  alternatives={[
    { option: 'CrewAI', reason: 'Higher abstraction but less control over state. Agent-to-agent calls add latency and complicate debugging.' },
    { option: 'AutoGen', reason: 'Great for conversation-style agents; poor fit for pipeline/assembly workloads.' },
    { option: 'Custom asyncio', reason: 'Maximum control but rebuilds graph plumbing from scratch — not worth the effort.' },
  ]}
/>

## Options Compared

<DecisionTable
  options={[
    {
      name: 'LangGraph',
      chosen: true,
      pros: ['Explicit state TypedDict', 'Fine-grained node control', 'No hosted runtime', 'Mature ecosystem'],
      cons: ['Verbose graph setup', 'Frequent API changes'],
    },
    {
      name: 'CrewAI',
      pros: ['Quick to scaffold', 'Role-based abstraction'],
      cons: ['Less state control', 'Agent-to-agent calls add latency', 'Harder to test'],
    },
    {
      name: 'AutoGen',
      pros: ['Great for conversational agents', 'Multi-modal support'],
      cons: ['Poor fit for pipeline workloads', 'Complex configuration'],
    },
  ]}
/>

<InfoPanel type="note" title="Revisit in Q3 2026">
  If the pipeline needs parallelism (e.g. Phase 1 agents running concurrently), consider
  switching to LangGraph's <code>Send</code> API or introducing a fan-out/fan-in pattern.
  Track in GitHub issue #42.
</InfoPanel>
